stories.title: Register Credit Coach Agent on Envoy Platform
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want the Credit Coach registered as a specialist agent on the Envoy platform, so that the orchestrator can route credit-related queries to it.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Credit Coach agent manifest is deployed, When a customer asks a credit-related question via the FA, Then the orchestrator routes the query to the Credit Coach agent within 500ms
Given the Credit Coach agent is registered, When it is published to the internal marketplace, Then it is available for reuse across Lloyds, Halifax, and Bank of Scotland brands
Given the Credit Coach agent is unavailable, When the circuit breaker triggers, Then a graceful error message is returned to the orchestrator within 2 seconds
Given the agent is deployed, When the orchestrator receives a non-credit query, Then it is NOT routed to the Credit Coach agent
stories.reasoning: Foundational infrastructure story — without orchestrator routing, no credit queries reach the agent. Includes circuit breaker for resilience per EA Section 9.

stories.title: Configure Intent Classification for Credit Queries
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want intent classification rules configured for credit-related queries, so that the orchestrator accurately distinguishes credit queries from other financial queries.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer types 'What is my credit score?', When the orchestrator classifies the intent, Then it is routed to the Credit Coach agent with >95% accuracy
Given an ambiguous query like 'How am I doing financially?', When the orchestrator cannot determine intent with high confidence, Then it asks a clarifying question before routing
Given a non-credit query like 'What did I spend on groceries?', When the orchestrator classifies it, Then it is routed to the Spending Agent, not Credit Coach
stories.reasoning: Separate from registration as it involves NLP model configuration and testing for accurate routing.

stories.title: Capture Explicit CRA Consent with Transparency Disclosures
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want to provide explicit consent before my credit data is retrieved, so that I understand what data is accessed, why, and how it will be used.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have not previously consented, When I access the Credit Coach for the first time, Then I am presented with a consent screen explaining: data retrieved (score + factors only), CRA name, purpose (coaching only), no marketing use, and automated processing details
Given the consent screen is displayed, When I review it, Then no checkboxes are pre-ticked and I must actively opt-in per CRA
Given I provide consent, When the system records it, Then the consent record includes timestamp, consent text version, and customer ID and is retained indefinitely
Given I am using a screen reader, When the consent screen is displayed, Then all elements are accessible with ARIA labels and the content is navigable via VoiceOver/TalkBack
Given consent data is stored, When accessed by any system component, Then it is encrypted at rest using AES-256 and transmitted via TLS 1.3
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR Art. 6 (lawful basis), Art. 13(2)(f) (transparency), CONC 5.2A.15R

requirements_used: FR-29 — Explicit Consent Capture for CRA Data
FR-40 — Transparency at Point of Data Collection
FR-43 — Purpose Limitation for CRA Data
domain_kb_sections: PD3 — Soft Search vs Hard Search

stories.reasoning: Legal prerequisite — no CRA data retrieval without explicit consent. Combines consent capture, transparency, and purpose limitation into a single user-testable flow.

stories.title: Manage Consent Withdrawal and Re-consent
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want to withdraw my CRA consent at any time, so that my data is no longer retrieved and I maintain control over my personal information.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have previously consented, When I navigate to Settings > Credit Coach > Manage Consent, Then I can withdraw consent for each CRA independently
Given I withdraw consent, When the withdrawal is processed, Then CRA data retrieval stops immediately and a deletion workflow is triggered for stored CRA data
Given the consent text version changes, When I next access the Credit Coach, Then I am prompted to re-consent with the updated text before any data is retrieved
Given consent withdrawal is processed, When the system updates records, Then the consent record is marked as withdrawn (not deleted) with withdrawal timestamp for compliance
Then all interactive elements are navigable via VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android) with logical focus order
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR Art. 7(3) (right to withdraw consent)

requirements_used: FR-29 — Explicit Consent Capture for CRA Data
FR-44 — Data Minimisation for CRA Retrieval

stories.reasoning: Consent withdrawal is a distinct user action from consent capture, with different system behaviours (deletion workflow, re-consent triggers).

stories.title: Implement Accessible Credit Coach UI Component Library
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want the Credit Coach to use consistent, accessible UI components, so that I can interact with the service regardless of visual ability or device.
acceptance_criteria: Given the design system is implemented, When any Credit Coach screen is rendered, Then all text meets 4.5:1 contrast ratio and UI components meet 3:1 contrast ratio per WCAG 2.1 AA
Given a colour-blind user views score visualisations, When the gauge or chart is displayed, Then information is conveyed via shape/pattern in addition to colour using ColorBrewer safe palettes

Given a screen reader user navigates the Credit Coach, When interacting with score gauge, trend chart, action card, or alert card components, Then each has appropriate ARIA labels and is fully operable via VoiceOver/TalkBack
Given the app is running on any brand (Lloyds/Halifax/BoS), When Credit Coach components render, Then brand-specific theming is applied without affecting accessibility compliance
stories.regulatory_linkage: Equality Act 2010 (reasonable adjustments)
stories.reasoning: Shared components enable consistent accessible UI across all features. NFR-11/12/13 mandate accessibility from day one. Large story due to multiple component types and multi-brand theming.

stories.title: Provision CRA Data Domain with Encryption and IAM Isolation
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want CRA data stored in a separated, encrypted data domain with strict IAM isolation, so that credit data is protected and cannot be co-mingled with bank transaction data.
acceptance_criteria: Given the CRA data domain is provisioned, When CRA score data is stored in Cloud SQL, Then it is encrypted at rest using AES-256 with CMEK via Cloud KMS
Given IAM policies are configured, When any service outside the CRA domain attempts to access CRA data directly, Then the request is denied by IAM policy
Given data retention policies are applied, When score data reaches 6 years post-service-closure, Then it is automatically archived to Cloud Storage Coldline
Given raw CRA API responses are stored temporarily, When the next score refresh completes successfully, Then raw responses from the previous cycle are purged
Given CRA data is classified Restricted, When any access occurs, Then it is logged in Cloud Audit Logs with full request context
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR Art. 5(1)(f) (integrity and confidentiality), FCA SYSC 9.1.1R (6-year retention)

requirements_used: FR-42 — Data Separation Between Bank and CRA Data

stories.reasoning: Infrastructure must exist before CRA data can be stored. FR-42 mandates separation; NFRs mandate encryption and retention. Security AC included for Restricted data.

stories.title: Configure Pub/Sub Topics and Dataflow Pipeline for Score Events
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want Pub/Sub topics and Dataflow pipelines configured for credit score events, so that downstream services (alerts, analytics) can react to score changes in real-time.
acceptance_criteria: Given a score refresh completes, When a score change is detected, Then a score-change event is published to the designated Pub/Sub topic within 1 second
Given events are published to Pub/Sub, When Dataflow processes them, Then they are written to BigQuery for analytics within 5 minutes
Given the pipeline is running, When processing 10M+ customer score events in a batch window, Then throughput does not degrade below 99th percentile SLA
Then CRA data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3), and is not included in application logs or error messages

requirements_used: FR-42 — Data Separation Between Bank and CRA Data

stories.reasoning: Event infrastructure is a separate deployable unit from the data domain itself. Required for alerts (EP-07) and analytics.

stories.title: Validate Design System Components Against Multi-Brand Theming
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want the design system components validated across all three brands, so that accessibility and visual consistency are maintained regardless of brand context.
acceptance_criteria: Given the component library is deployed, When rendered under Lloyds, Halifax, and Bank of Scotland themes, Then all components pass automated axe-core accessibility checks
Given brand theming is applied, When colour palettes change per brand, Then contrast ratios remain >= 4.5:1 for text and >= 3:1 for UI components across all themes
Given loading and error states exist, When a component enters loading or error state, Then the state is communicated via ARIA live regions for screen reader users
stories.regulatory_linkage: Equality Act 2010 (reasonable adjustments)
stories.reasoning: Validation story ensuring multi-brand theming doesn't break accessibility. Separate from component creation.

stories.title: Retrieve Credit Score from CRA via Soft Search
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want my credit score retrieved from the CRA without leaving a footprint on my credit file, so that monitoring my score does not harm my creditworthiness.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have provided explicit CRA consent, When the system retrieves my score, Then it uses a soft search that leaves no visible footprint on my credit file
Given the CRA API is called, When a valid response is returned, Then the score, band, and contributing factors are stored in the CRA data domain within 3 seconds end-to-end
Given the CRA API is unavailable, When the circuit breaker triggers after 2.5s timeout, Then cached stale data is shown with a 'Last updated [date]' timestamp
Given CRA data is returned, When the data quality score is below 95% or data is older than 30 days, Then the data is flagged for re-fetch on the next cycle
Given CRA data is classified Restricted, When it is transmitted between services, Then TLS 1.3 is enforced on all connections
Then all touch targets meet minimum 48dp size and text respects system font scale setting
stories.regulatory_linkage: CONC 5.2A (soft search basis)

requirements_used: FR-01 — Credit Score Retrieval on Opt-In
domain_kb_sections: PD3 — Soft Search vs Hard Search

stories.reasoning: Core data retrieval service. All dashboard features depend on this. Soft search is critical — hard search would damage customer credit.

stories.title: Display Credit Score with Band Classification
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want to see my current credit score, score band, and month-on-month change, so that I understand my credit health at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Given my score has been retrieved, When I view the Credit Coach dashboard, Then I see my current score on a gauge widget, my score band (Poor/Fair/Good/Excellent), and month-on-month change with directional indicator (+/-/unchanged)
Given this is my first score retrieval, When no previous score exists for comparison, Then the month-on-month change shows 'First check — no comparison available' instead of a delta
Given the CRA is Experian, When the score is displayed, Then it is clearly labelled 'Experian score (0-999)' so I understand the scale
Given I am using a screen reader, When the dashboard is displayed, Then the score value, band, and change direction are announced via accessible labels
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (consumer understanding outcome)

requirements_used: FR-02 — Display Current Score and Band

stories.reasoning: Primary user-facing display. FR-02 and FR-25 are the same screen — normalisation is a display concern.

stories.title: Handle Thin File and CRA Unavailability on Dashboard
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want clear messaging when my credit file is insufficient or the CRA is unavailable, so that I am not confused by missing data.
acceptance_criteria: Given the CRA returns a thin file indicator, When the dashboard renders, Then I see a message explaining 'We don't have enough data to generate a full score — here's how to build your credit history'

Given the CRA is unavailable and stale data exists, When the dashboard renders, Then stale data is shown with a warning banner stating 'Score as of [date] — we'll update this when the service is available'
Given the CRA is unavailable and no cached data exists, When the dashboard renders, Then a friendly error message is shown with a 'Try again later' option
Then score visualisations use patterns/shapes in addition to colour and meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio (4.5:1)
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions

requirements_used: FR-02 — Display Current Score and Band

stories.reasoning: Error path for the dashboard. Thin files and CRA unavailability are common edge cases that need explicit UX handling.

stories.title: Display Score Factors in Plain English
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want my credit score factors explained in plain English, so that I understand what is helping and hurting my score without needing financial expertise.
acceptance_criteria: Given my score factors are available, When I view the factors section, Then I see up to 5 positive and 5 negative factors ordered by impact magnitude, each with category, impact direction, and plain language explanation (Flesch reading ease >= 60)
Given a factor is displayed, When I tap 'why this matters', Then a tooltip explains the factor's significance in plain English
Given the CRA returns a factor code not in the plain-English mapping, When the factor is displayed, Then it shows the raw factor with a 'Contact us for explanation' fallback
Given I am using a screen reader, When navigating factors, Then each factor's category, direction, and explanation are announced in logical reading order
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (consumer understanding), PRA FS2/23 (explainability)

requirements_used: FR-03 — Display Score Factors in Plain English

stories.reasoning: Standalone display feature. Plain English requirement aligns with Consumer Duty understanding outcome and FS2/23 explainability.

stories.title: Explain Score Changes Between Refreshes
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want to understand why my credit score changed, so that I can take informed action to improve or maintain it.
acceptance_criteria: Given my score has changed since the last refresh, When I view the change explanation, Then I see previous score, new score, delta, and a ranked list of factors that changed with specific details (e.g., 'Credit utilisation increased from 45% to 62%')
Given my score dropped, When the explanation is displayed, Then the tone is empathetic with no alarm language, and an actionable next step is suggested
Given my score increased, When the explanation is displayed, Then positive reinforcement messaging is shown
Given my score has not changed, When I view the dashboard, Then a reassurance message is displayed (e.g., 'Your score is stable — keep up the good work')
Given I am using a screen reader, When the change explanation is displayed, Then the delta direction and contributing factors are announced accessibly
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (consumer understanding)
stories.reasoning: Distinct from static factor display — this is delta analysis triggered by score refresh events.

stories.title: Store Score History for Trend Analysis
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want each score data point stored with full context, so that trend visualisation and regulatory retention requirements are met.
acceptance_criteria: Given a score refresh completes, When the score is stored, Then the record includes: score value, band, date, CRA source, and contributing factors snapshot
Given score history is queried, When a date range and CRA source filter are applied, Then the API returns the correct time-series data within 1 second
Given a score record reaches 6 years post-service-closure, When the retention policy runs, Then it is archived to Cloud Storage Coldline (not deleted)
Given score history data is classified Restricted, When stored in Cloud SQL, Then it is encrypted at rest using AES-256
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA SYSC 9.1.1R (6-year retention)

requirements_used: FR-04 — Store Score History for Trend Visualisation

stories.reasoning: Data persistence layer for trend visualisation (rendered in EP-04). Storage must exist in Sprint 1 to begin accumulating history.

stories.title: Answer Conversational Credit Queries
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want to ask natural language questions about my credit health, so that I can get answers without navigating through multiple screens.
acceptance_criteria: Given I type 'What is my credit score?', When the Credit Coach processes the query, Then it responds conversationally with my current score, band, and last refresh date
Given I ask 'Why did my score change?', When the agent processes the query, Then it provides the score change explanation with contributing factors in conversational format

Given I ask an unsupported question (e.g., 'Can you transfer money?'), When the agent cannot handle it, Then it responds with 'I can help with credit score, factors, and improvement tips. For other queries, I'll connect you with the right assistant'
Given I am using a screen reader, When the conversational response is displayed, Then it is announced in full via assistive technology
stories.reasoning: FR-26 is the conversational interface layer over the dashboard data. Primary interaction model for the FA.

stories.title: Handle Ambiguous and Out-of-Scope Conversational Queries
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want the Credit Coach to ask clarifying questions when my query is ambiguous, so that I get accurate and relevant responses.
acceptance_criteria: Given I ask an ambiguous query like 'How am I doing?', When the agent cannot determine intent with high confidence, Then it asks a clarifying question (e.g., 'Would you like to know about your credit score or your spending?')
Given I input offensive or harmful content, When the agent detects it, Then standard guardrails apply and the input is deflected gracefully without processing
Given CRA data is unavailable during conversation, When I ask about my score, Then the agent informs me and offers to retry or check back later
Then all form inputs have associated labels readable by screen readers
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.reasoning: Error and edge-case handling for conversational interface. Separate from happy path as it involves different NLP logic.

stories.title: Schedule Monthly Score Refresh for All Enrolled Customers
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want a background scheduler that triggers monthly CRA score refreshes for all enrolled customers, so that scores remain current without manual intervention.
acceptance_criteria: Given 10M+ customers are enrolled, When the monthly refresh batch runs, Then all customers are refreshed within the configured batch window without system degradation
Given CRA rate limits exist, When the batch executes, Then requests are staggered to avoid exceeding CRA API rate limits
Given a refresh fails for a customer, When the retry logic executes, Then it retries with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts before marking as failed
Given a refresh completes, When a score change is detected, Then a Pub/Sub event is emitted for downstream consumers (alerts, analytics)
stories.reasoning: Operational scheduling distinct from the retrieval service itself. Handles scale (10M+ customers) and CRA rate limiting.

stories.title: Admin Configuration for Refresh Frequency
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want to configure score refresh frequency per CRA agreement, so that I can adjust scheduling without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given I access the admin configuration UI, When I adjust the refresh frequency for a CRA, Then the change takes effect on the next batch cycle
Given frequency is configurable, When I set it, Then it enforces minimum monthly and maximum weekly boundaries per CRA agreement
Given a configuration change is made, When it is saved, Then an audit log entry records who changed what and when
stories.reasoning: Separate admin capability from the scheduler itself. Allows ops team to adjust without deployments.

stories.title: Validate CRA Data Quality Before Processing
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want CRA data validated for quality and freshness before it drives recommendations, so that stale or incorrect data does not mislead customers.
acceptance_criteria: Given CRA data is received, When the quality check runs, Then data with quality score < 95% is rejected and flagged for re-fetch
Given CRA data is received, When the freshness check runs, Then data older than 30 days is rejected and a re-fetch is triggered
Given data fails quality validation, When the failure is logged, Then it is recorded in Cloud Monitoring with alert to ops team if failure rate exceeds 5%
stories.regulatory_linkage: PRA FS2/23 (data quality for AI systems)

requirements_used: FR-01 — Credit Score Retrieval on Opt-In

stories.reasoning: NFR-25 mandates data quality validation. Separate from retrieval as it's a validation gate that can reject data.

stories.title: Expand All Score Factors with See-All Option
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to see all my score factors beyond the top 5, so that I have a complete picture of what influences my score.
acceptance_criteria: Given more than 5 positive or 5 negative factors exist, When I tap 'See all', Then the full list of factors is expanded with the same formatting and accessibility
Given the expanded list is shown, When I scroll through factors, Then they remain ordered by impact magnitude
Given I am using a screen reader, When I activate 'See all', Then the expansion is announced and new factors are navigable
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions

requirements_used: FR-03 — Display Score Factors in Plain English

stories.reasoning: Enhancement to the factor display — the 'see all' expansion is a separate interaction from the initial 5+5 display.

stories.title: Handle CRA API Unavailability Gracefully
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to see a helpful message when the CRA is unavailable, so that I understand why my score cannot be displayed and know when to try again.
acceptance_criteria: Given the CRA API is unavailable or times out (>2.5s), When I navigate to the score dashboard, Then I see a cached score (if available) with 'Last updated [date]' label and a 'Refresh' button
Given no cached score exists and CRA is unavailable, When I navigate to the score dashboard, Then I see 'We couldn't retrieve your score right now. Please try again later.' with a retry button
Given the CRA returns a partial response (score but no factors), When the dashboard loads, Then the score is displayed and factors section shows 'Factor details temporarily unavailable'

Given the circuit breaker has tripped (3 consecutive failures), When I attempt to refresh, Then the system waits for the circuit breaker reset period before retrying and informs me of approximate wait time
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (consumer support outcome — customers must not face unreasonable barriers)

requirements_used: FR-01 — Credit Score Retrieval on Opt-In
architecture_kb_sections: Section 3.2 — Backend Architecture (circuit breakers for CRA calls)

stories.reasoning: Error path for CRA unavailability. Enterprise architecture KB mandates circuit breakers for external CRA calls. Cached fallback provides graceful degradation per Consumer Duty (consumer support outcome). 3 points: cache logic + circuit breaker integration + UI states.

stories.title: Generate Ranked Improvement Actions from Credit Profile
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want a personalised ranked list of credit improvement actions based on my specific profile, so that I know the most impactful steps to improve my score.
acceptance_criteria: Given my credit profile has been retrieved, When the improvement plan is generated via Vertex AI, Then I see up to 10 actions ranked by estimated impact (highest first), each with title, plain English description, category, estimated point impact range, timeframe, and difficulty level
Given an action is displayed, When I tap 'why this helps', Then an explanation of why this specific action benefits my profile is shown (Flesch reading ease >= 60)
Given recommendations are generated, When reviewed for bias, Then they genuinely benefit me and are not biased toward Lloyds lending products
Given I am using a screen reader, When the action list is displayed, Then each action's rank, title, impact, and timeframe are announced in logical order
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (act in good faith), PRA SS1/23 (model risk), PRA FS2/23 (explainability)

requirements_used: FR-08 — Estimated Point Impact and Timeframe per Action

stories.reasoning: Core coaching capability. FR-07, FR-08, FR-38 are inseparable — the generation engine must satisfy all three simultaneously. Large story due to ML model integration.

stories.title: Handle Perfect Credit and Low-Confidence Recommendations
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want appropriate messaging when no improvements are needed or when recommendations have low confidence, so that I am not misled by uncertain advice.
acceptance_criteria: Given my credit profile has no improvement opportunities, When the plan is generated, Then I see a congratulatory message with maintenance tips instead of an empty action list
Given the Vertex AI model returns low-confidence recommendations, When they are displayed, Then they are flagged as 'suggestions' with lower visual prominence and a caveat about uncertainty
Given I am using a screen reader, When the congratulatory or low-confidence message is displayed, Then it is announced accessibly
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.regulatory_linkage: PRA FS2/23 (explainability — confidence disclosure)
stories.reasoning: Edge cases for the recommendation engine. Perfect credit and low confidence are distinct from the happy path.

stories.title: Track Improvement Action Completion
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to track progress on my improvement actions, so that I can see how far I've come and what remains.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have active improvement actions, When I view my plan, Then each action shows its state (Not Started, In Progress, Completed, Skipped) and overall progress percentage
Given an action's completion is detectable automatically (e.g., utilisation drop on next refresh), When the condition is met, Then the action is automatically marked as Completed
Given I manually confirm an action as complete, When I mark it done, Then it moves to the completed archive and the plan recalculates remaining actions
Given I am using a screen reader, When navigating the action list, Then each action's state and the overall progress percentage are announced
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.reasoning: Distinct interaction pattern from generation — this is ongoing state management and progress UI.

stories.title: Celebrate Credit Milestones
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to be celebrated when I reach credit milestones, so that I feel motivated to continue improving my score.
acceptance_criteria: Given my score crosses a band threshold (e.g., Fair to Good), When the milestone is detected on refresh, Then I see an animated celebration (badge/confetti) with a congratulatory message summarising my achievement
Given a trivial score change occurs (< 5 points within the same band), When the refresh completes, Then no celebration is triggered
Given multiple milestones are achieved simultaneously, When celebrations are triggered, Then the highest-impact milestone is celebrated prominently and others are listed
Given I have notifications disabled, When a milestone is achieved, Then the celebration is shown on my next app open instead of via push notification
Given I am using a screen reader, When a celebration is displayed, Then the achievement summary is announced without relying on visual animation
stories.reasoning: Engagement feature — separate from tracking as it's a notification/UI overlay triggered by events.

stories.title: Dynamically Update Improvement Plan on Profile Change
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want my improvement plan to update automatically when my credit profile changes, so that my actions remain relevant.
acceptance_criteria: Given my credit profile changes (new score refresh, action completed, new account detected), When the plan recalculates, Then actions are re-ranked, irrelevant actions removed, and new actions added as appropriate
Given the plan has been updated, When I view it, Then I see a notification explaining what changed and why (e.g., 'Your utilisation dropped — we've updated your priorities')
Given a plan update occurs, When the previous version is archived, Then it is retained for audit purposes
Given recalculation frequency, When multiple triggers occur within one refresh cycle, Then the plan recalculates at most once per refresh to avoid churn
Then status changes are announced to assistive technology via live regions
stories.regulatory_linkage: PRA SS1/23 (model governance — plan versioning)
stories.reasoning: Distinct from initial generation — this is the reactive update mechanism ensuring plans stay relevant.

stories.title: Connect Spending Insights to Credit Impact
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to see how my spending patterns affect my credit and affordability, so that I can make informed financial decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an active improvement action related to affordability, When spending data from the FA Spending Agent is available, Then contextual tips are shown (e.g., 'Reducing dining by £150/month improves affordability by 12%')
Given spending data is combined with credit data, When displayed, Then the combination occurs only in the presentation layer and is never co-stored
Given a spending tip is shown, When I tap it, Then a plain English explanation of the credit/affordability connection is displayed
Given I am using a screen reader, When spending-to-credit tips are displayed, Then they are announced with full context
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions

domain_kb_sections: PD3 — Affordability Assessment disposable income calculation

stories.reasoning: Cross-agent integration feature connecting spending data to credit coaching. Enriches improvement actions with actionable spending context.

stories.title: Conduct Quarterly Bias Audit on Recommendation Model
stories.user_story: As a compliance officer, I want quarterly bias audits conducted on the improvement recommendation model, so that we can demonstrate non-discrimination across protected characteristics.
acceptance_criteria: Given the recommendation model is in production, When the quarterly audit runs, Then it assesses outcomes across age, gender, ethnicity, and disability groups
Given audit results are generated, When the disparate impact ratio is calculated, Then it must be > 0.8 (four-fifths rule) for all protected groups
Given a bias breach is detected (ratio <= 0.8), When reported, Then remediation must be completed within 30 days and reported to the model risk committee
Then all interactive elements are navigable via VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android) with logical focus order
stories.regulatory_linkage: Equality Act 2010, PRA SS1/23 (model risk), PRA FS2/23 (fairness)
stories.reasoning: NFR-21 mandates quarterly bias audits. Separate from recommendation generation as it's a governance process.

stories.title: View Completed Action History
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to view my history of completed improvement actions, so that I can see my progress over time.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have completed actions, When I navigate to 'Completed actions', Then I see a chronological list of all completed and skipped actions with completion dates
Given a completed action is displayed, When I view its details, Then I see the original estimated impact and the actual score change that occurred after completion
Given I am using a screen reader, When navigating the completed actions list, Then each action's title, completion date, and outcome are announced
stories.reasoning: Archive view for completed actions. Separate from active tracking as it's a historical reference feature.

stories.title: Handle Low-Confidence or Failed Action Plan Generation
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want clear communication when the system cannot generate reliable improvement actions, so that I am not misled by uncertain advice.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Vertex AI model returns recommendations with confidence below threshold (<60%), When the action plan is displayed, Then actions are labelled 'Suggestions' (not 'Actions') with a disclaimer 'These are estimates based on limited data'

Given the model fails to generate any recommendations (timeout or error), When I view the action plan section, Then I see 'We need more data to generate your plan. Check back after your next score refresh.' with a link to the score dashboard

Given my credit profile is 'thin file' (limited credit history), When the plan is generated, Then the system prioritises credit-building actions (e.g., 'Consider a credit-builder card') over reduction actions
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.regulatory_linkage: PRA FS2/23 (explainability — confidence disclosure), FCA Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm — don't mislead with uncertain advice)

domain_kb_sections: PD3 — Credit Score Factors (thin file handling)

stories.reasoning: Error/edge case path for action plan generation. FS2/23 requires explainability including confidence levels. Thin file scenario from GAP-10 in requirements. 3 points: confidence threshold logic + thin file detection + fallback UI.

stories.title: Display Interactive Score Trend Chart
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to see my credit score trend over time on an interactive chart, so that I can visualise my progress and identify patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have at least 2 months of score history, When I view the trend chart, Then I see an interactive line chart with selectable ranges (3m, 6m, 12m, 24m) defaulting to 12 months
Given I tap a data point on the chart, When the detail popover appears, Then I see the score value, band, and key factor at that point in time
Given the chart uses colours, When rendered, Then it uses a colour-blind safe palette (ColorBrewer) with pattern differentiation and meets 4.5:1 contrast ratio
Given I am using a screen reader, When I access the trend chart, Then a data table alternative is available with the same information in tabular format
Given I have less than 2 months of history, When the chart renders, Then available data is shown with a 'More data coming soon' message
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions

requirements_used: FR-27 — Score History and Trend Visualisation
FR-04 — Store Score History for Trend Visualisation

stories.reasoning: FR-27 renders the data stored by FR-04 (EP-02). Sprint 2 delivers the visual layer over Sprint 1's data accumulation.

stories.title: Annotate Significant Events on Trend Chart
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want significant events annotated on my score trend chart, so that I can understand what caused score movements.
acceptance_criteria: Given a significant event occurred (action completed, new account opened, score band change), When the trend chart renders, Then an annotation marker appears at the corresponding date
Given I tap an annotation marker, When the detail appears, Then I see a description of the event (e.g., 'Action completed: Reduced credit utilisation below 30%')
Given a score gap exists (customer opted out for a period), When the chart renders, Then the gap is clearly shown with no interpolation and a 'No data for this period' label
Then all touch targets meet minimum 48dp size and text respects system font scale setting
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions

requirements_used: FR-27 — Score History and Trend Visualisation

stories.reasoning: Annotations add context to the trend chart, helping customers correlate actions with score movements.

stories.title: Display Cross-Lender Debt Visibility
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to see my total debt across all lenders from CRA data, so that I understand my complete financial picture.
acceptance_criteria: Given CRA data includes debt information, When I view the debt visibility section, Then I see total outstanding debt, number of active credit accounts, and breakdown by type (mortgage, personal loan, credit card, overdraft, HP/PCP)
Given individual accounts are listed, When I view an account, Then I see lender name, outstanding balance, credit limit (if revolving), monthly payment, and account status
Given the CRA returns incomplete debt data, When the section renders, Then available data is shown with a disclaimer 'Some accounts may not be shown — CRA data may not include all lenders'
Given I dispute a debt shown, When I look for resolution options, Then a link to the CRA dispute process is provided (the system does not remove the entry)
Given debt data is classified Restricted, When accessed, Then it is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest (AES-256)
Then score visualisations use patterns/shapes in addition to colour and meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio (4.5:1)
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR (data accuracy — signpost dispute process)
stories.reasoning: Separate feature requiring additional CRA data fields beyond score/factors. High value for coaching context.

stories.title: Handle Disputed and Closed Account Debt Display
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to understand why closed accounts still appear in my debt view and how to dispute inaccuracies, so that I can take appropriate action.
acceptance_criteria: Given a closed account still shows on CRA data, When I view it, Then an explanation of CRA retention periods (6 years) is provided
Given I want to dispute a debt entry, When I tap 'Dispute this', Then I am directed to the relevant CRA's dispute process with clear instructions
Given I am using a screen reader, When dispute links and retention explanations are displayed, Then they are announced with full context
Then CRA data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3), and is not included in application logs or error messages
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR Art. 16 (right to rectification — via CRA)
stories.reasoning: Error/edge case for debt visibility. Disputes and closed accounts are common customer confusion points.

stories.title: Simulate Score Impact for Debt Paydown and Utilisation Scenarios
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to simulate what happens to my score if I pay down debt or reduce utilisation, so that I can make informed decisions about managing my credit.
acceptance_criteria: Given I select a 'pay down debt' scenario specifying amount and account, When the simulation runs via Vertex AI, Then I receive an estimated new score, point delta, confidence level (High/Medium/Low), and timeframe within 5 seconds
Given I select a 'reduce utilisation' scenario with a target percentage, When the simulation completes, Then the result shows estimated score impact with contributing factor explanation
Given the model has been validated per PRA SS1/23, When deployed, Then it is registered in the model registry with risk classification and validation date
Given simulation data is processed, When the model is invoked, Then CRA data quality is validated (>= 95%, < 30 days old) before simulation proceeds
Then all form inputs have associated labels readable by screen readers
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.regulatory_linkage: PRA SS1/23 (model risk management)

requirements_used: FR-13 — Simulation Returns Score Impact and Confidence

stories.reasoning: Core simulation engine for the most common scenarios (debt paydown and utilisation). Vertex AI model deployment with PRA compliance.

stories.title: Simulate Score Impact for Account Changes and Missed Payments
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to simulate what happens if I close accounts, open new credit, or miss payments, so that I understand the consequences before acting.
acceptance_criteria: Given I select 'close account' specifying which account, When the simulation runs, Then I see estimated score impact including the effect on credit history length and credit mix
Given I select 'open new credit' specifying type and amount, When the simulation completes, Then the result includes a warning about the impact of a hard search and additional debt
Given I select 'missed payment' specifying number and recency, When the simulation runs, Then the result shows estimated score impact with the 35% payment history weighting context
Given model performance drift exceeds 5% from baseline, When monitoring detects it, Then a mandatory review is triggered and flagged to the model risk committee
Then status changes are announced to assistive technology via live regions
stories.regulatory_linkage: PRA SS1/23 (model drift monitoring)

requirements_used: FR-13 — Simulation Returns Score Impact and Confidence

stories.reasoning: Remaining simulation scenarios (close, open, miss). Separated from US-027 for manageable scope while covering all FR-12 scenarios.

stories.title: Display Simulation Results with Disclaimers
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want simulation results clearly presented with appropriate disclaimers, so that I understand these are estimates and not guarantees.
acceptance_criteria: Given a simulation completes, When results are displayed, Then I see current score, simulated score, delta, confidence indicator, and estimated timeframe with a visual before/after gauge comparison
Given results are shown, When the disclaimer is rendered, Then 'Estimate only — not a guarantee of future score changes' is always visible (not hidden behind an expand) in text meeting 4.5:1 contrast
Given the confidence level is Low, When results are displayed, Then an additional caveat is shown explaining the estimate has higher uncertainty
Given I am using a screen reader, When results are displayed, Then the current score, simulated score, delta, confidence, and disclaimer are all announced in logical order
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (consumer understanding — no misleading estimates)

requirements_used: FR-14 — Label Simulation Results as Estimates

stories.reasoning: Presentation and compliance labelling layer. FR-14 specifically about labelling as estimates — separate from the engine.

stories.title: Accept Natural Language Simulation Input
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to describe simulation scenarios in my own words, so that I don't need to navigate complex forms to run a what-if analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Given I type 'What if I pay off my overdraft?', When the NLP parses my input, Then it extracts scenario type (pay down debt), identifies the overdraft account, and runs the simulation

Given I type an ambiguous query like 'What happens if I close a card?', When the system cannot determine which account, Then it asks a clarifying question ('Which card would you like to simulate closing?')

Given I describe an unsupported scenario (e.g., 'What if I win the lottery?'), When the system cannot map it, Then it responds with 'I can simulate these types of changes: paying down debt, closing accounts, opening new credit, missed payments, and reducing utilisation'
Given I am using a screen reader, When the conversational simulation interface is active, Then all prompts, clarifications, and results are announced accessibly
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions

architecture_kb_sections: Section 2.3 — Agentic Architecture NL to code

stories.reasoning: Distinct NLP layer converting free text to structured simulation parameters. Separate from engine as it's a UI/UX concern.

stories.title: Handle Simulation Engine Timeout and Invalid Scenarios
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want clear feedback when a simulation cannot be completed, so that I understand the limitation and can try a different scenario.
acceptance_criteria: Given I submit a simulation scenario, When the Vertex AI model times out (>5s), Then I see 'This simulation is taking longer than expected. Try a simpler scenario or check back later.' with a retry option
Given I enter a contradictory scenario (e.g., 'close all accounts AND open new credit'), When I submit, Then the system identifies the conflict and asks me to choose one action at a time

Given I enter a scenario the model cannot simulate (unsupported action type), When I submit, Then I see 'We can't simulate that scenario yet. Try: paying down debt, closing accounts, or reducing utilisation.'
Then all interactive elements are navigable via VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android) with logical focus order
stories.regulatory_linkage: PRA SS1/23 (model risk — graceful failure handling)

requirements_used: FR-14 — Label Simulation Results as Estimates
architecture_kb_sections: Section 2.1 — Vertex AI Foundation (model inference timeout)

stories.reasoning: Error path for simulation engine. NFR-02 specifies <5s response time — this story handles the timeout case. Contradictory scenario detection prevents confusing results. 3 points: timeout handling + conflict detection + fallback UI.

stories.title: Match Customer Profile Against Lending Eligibility Criteria
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want the system to match customer credit profiles against Lloyds lending product eligibility criteria on each refresh, so that only qualifying products are surfaced.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer's score is refreshed, When the eligibility matching runs as a background process, Then it checks credit score thresholds, adverse markers, debt-to-income ratio, and existing exposure per product
Given a customer does not meet eligibility criteria for a product, When the FA displays lending products, Then that product is NOT shown to the customer (pre-qualification gate)
Given eligibility results are generated, When cached, Then they remain valid until the next score refresh and are not shown as offers (soft assessment only)
Given eligibility data is classified Restricted, When processed and stored, Then it is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3)
stories.regulatory_linkage: CONC 5.2A (creditworthiness — soft pre-qualification)

requirements_used: FR-16 — Match Customer Profile Against Lending Criteria
FR-45 — Pre-Qualification Gate for FA Product Recommendations

stories.reasoning: FR-16 and FR-45 are the same engine — matching determines what to show/hide. Acts as pre-qualification gate.

stories.title: Generate Guaranteed Pre-Approved Offers with Affordability Assessment
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want pre-approved offers that are guaranteed so I won't be declined, so that I can apply with confidence.
acceptance_criteria: Given I qualify for a product, When a pre-approved offer is generated, Then it includes product name, personal APR, representative APR, total amount payable, monthly repayment, term, and credit limit (if revolving)
Given an offer is generated, When affordability is assessed per CONC 5.2A, Then it covers both credit risk AND ability to repay without hardship, stress tested at +3pp
Given an offer is generated, When the validity period is set, Then it is clearly stated (14-30 days configurable) and a real-time re-check occurs at application if >7 days since generation
Given the customer applies within the validity period, When the application is submitted, Then the customer will NOT be declined (guarantee honoured)
Given offer data is classified Restricted, When generated and stored, Then it is encrypted and access-logged per security policy
Then all interactive elements are navigable via VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android) with logical focus order
stories.regulatory_linkage: CONC 5.2A (affordability), CCA s.55B (creditworthiness)

requirements_used: FR-35 — Affordability Assessment for Pre-Approved Offers

stories.reasoning: FR-17 and FR-35 are inseparable — an offer cannot be guaranteed without proper affordability assessment. Large story due to regulatory complexity.

stories.title: Present Offers Contextually with Financial Promotion Compliance
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want pre-approved offers presented at appropriate moments with clear cost information, so that I can make informed borrowing decisions without feeling pressured.
acceptance_criteria: Given a pre-approved offer exists, When a contextually appropriate moment occurs (score improvement milestone, simulation shows consolidation benefit), Then the offer card is displayed showing product name, personal rate, monthly cost, total cost, and key benefit

Given the offer is a financial promotion, When displayed, Then representative APR is more prominent than any other rate, a representative example is included, and risks are given equal prominence to benefits per CONC 3
Given the customer has just experienced a negative event (score drop, missed payment), When offers are evaluated for display, Then they are NOT presented at that moment
Given I am using a screen reader, When an offer card is displayed, Then all financial information including APR, total cost, and risk warnings are announced in logical order
stories.regulatory_linkage: CONC 3 (financial promotions — clear, fair, not misleading)

requirements_used: FR-34 — Financial Promotion Compliance for Offers

stories.reasoning: FR-18 and FR-34 are the same screen — presentation must be both contextual AND compliant.

stories.title: One-Tap Application with SECCI and Cooling-Off Communication
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want to apply for a pre-approved offer with minimal friction while receiving all legally required information, so that I can borrow responsibly with full awareness of my rights.
acceptance_criteria: Given I tap 'Apply' on a pre-approved offer, When the application flow starts, Then my data is pre-filled from existing Lloyds records (name, address, income, employment) for review and confirmation

Given I review my pre-filled data, When I proceed, Then the SECCI document is displayed containing: type of credit, total amount, duration, APR, total amount payable, repayment schedule, and right to withdraw
Given I accept the terms, When I confirm via biometric authentication (SCA), Then the application is submitted and a confirmation screen shows next steps and 14-day withdrawal instructions
Given the 14-day cooling-off right, When the confirmation is displayed, Then it clearly states I can cancel without giving a reason within 14 days of signing
Given I am using a screen reader, When navigating the application flow, Then all pre-filled data, SECCI content, and cooling-off information are fully accessible
Then CRA data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3), and is not included in application logs or error messages
stories.regulatory_linkage: CCA s.55A (SECCI), CCA s.66A (14-day cooling-off), PSD2 (SCA)

requirements_used: FR-19 — One-Tap Application with Pre-Filled Data

stories.reasoning: FR-19, FR-32, FR-33 are sequential steps in the same application flow. Large story due to regulatory requirements.

stories.title: Handle Application Errors and Data Accuracy Issues
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want clear guidance when my pre-filled data is incorrect or the application encounters an error, so that I can resolve issues without abandoning the process.
acceptance_criteria: Given my pre-filled data is incorrect, When I edit a field, Then the updated data is used for the application and the change is logged
Given the application submission fails (network error, system unavailable), When the error occurs, Then I see a clear message with a 'Retry' option and my progress is preserved

Given the real-time re-check at application (>7 days since offer generation) fails affordability, When the check completes, Then I am informed the offer is no longer available with an empathetic explanation
Then all touch targets meet minimum 48dp size and text respects system font scale setting
stories.regulatory_linkage: CONC 5.2A (real-time affordability re-check)

requirements_used: FR-19 — One-Tap Application with Pre-Filled Data

stories.reasoning: Error path for the application flow. Covers data correction, submission failures, and stale offer withdrawal.

stories.title: Maintain Offer Audit Trail for Regulatory Compliance
stories.user_story: As a compliance officer, I want a complete audit trail of all offers generated, presented, and actioned, so that I can demonstrate regulatory compliance during FCA audits.
acceptance_criteria: Given an offer is generated, When the audit log records it, Then it includes eligibility criteria met, timestamp, offer version, and customer ID
Given an offer is presented to a customer, When the event is logged, Then it records context/trigger, session ID, device, and timestamp
Given a customer takes action on an offer (viewed, dismissed, saved, applied, expired), When the action occurs, Then it is logged with timestamp and action type
Given a regulatory audit request, When the compliance team queries the audit trail, Then all records for a customer are retrievable within 24 hours
Given audit data retention, When records reach 6 years (FCA SYSC 9.1.1R) or 3 years for financial promotion records, Then the appropriate retention policy is applied
Then score visualisations use patterns/shapes in addition to colour and meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio (4.5:1)
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA SYSC 9.1.1R (6-year record keeping), CONC 3 (financial promotion records 3 years)

domain_kb_sections: PD22 — Financial Promotions record keeping 3 years

stories.reasoning: Separate audit/compliance feature. Critical for regulatory defence but distinct from offer logic.

stories.title: Implement Unified Recommendation Engine Arbitration
stories.user_story: As a system administrator, I want a unified recommendation engine that resolves conflicts between spending and credit health recommendations, so that customers receive consistent, non-contradictory advice.
acceptance_criteria: Given the FA Spending Agent suggests spending and the Credit Coach suggests saving, When a conflict is detected, Then the arbitration logic resolves it: credit health wins for at-risk customers (score below Fair band), spending wins for healthy-score customers
Given arbitration rules are applied, When a recommendation is surfaced, Then only the winning recommendation is shown to the customer (no contradictions)
Given the priority threshold is configurable, When the product team adjusts it, Then the change takes effect without code deployment
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm from conflicting advice)
stories.reasoning: Cross-agent coordination feature. Prevents conflicting recommendations between FA agents.

stories.title: Suppress Offer Presentation During Financial Distress Indicators
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer showing signs of financial difficulty, I want the system to withhold credit offers, so that I am not encouraged to take on debt I cannot afford.
acceptance_criteria: Given my account shows financial distress indicators (3+ missed payments, gambling transactions, payday loan usage detected via CRA data), When the offer engine runs, Then no pre-approved offers are generated or displayed

Given offers are suppressed due to distress indicators, When I view the Credit Coach dashboard, Then I see supportive messaging: 'We're here to help you improve your financial health' with links to StepChange (0800 138 1111) and Citizens Advice
Given I am in Breathing Space (Debt Respite Scheme), When the offer engine runs, Then all offers are suppressed for the duration of the 60-day moratorium plus 30 days
Then CRA data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3), and is not included in application logs or error messages
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm), CONC 7.3 (treat customers in difficulty fairly), Debt Respite Scheme Regulations 2020 (Breathing Space)

requirements_used: FR-37 — Suppress Offers During Financial Distress
FR-36 — Vulnerability Detection and Debt Advice Signposting
domain_kb_sections: PD15 — Vulnerable Customers (gambling markers, financial distress)

stories.reasoning: Critical compliance story combining offer suppression (FR-37), Breathing Space (FR-48), and vulnerability signposting (FR-36). Domain KB PD7 specifies 60-day moratorium. PD15 identifies gambling and payday loans as vulnerability indicators. 5 points: distress indicator detection + Breathing Space integration + suppression logic + supportive messaging.

stories.title: Alert on Credit Utilisation Threshold Breach
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to be alerted when my credit utilisation approaches dangerous thresholds, so that I can take action before my score is damaged.
acceptance_criteria: Given my credit utilisation crosses a threshold (default: 50% advisory, 75% warning, 90% urgent), When the threshold is breached, Then I receive a push notification and in-app inbox message within 15 minutes

Given the alert is delivered, When I view it, Then it shows current utilisation percentage, contributing accounts, estimated score impact if utilisation increases further, and a suggested action (e.g., 'Consider paying down your Visa card by £200 to drop below 75%')
Given I am flagged as in financial distress, When a utilisation threshold is breached, Then the alert is suppressed (no notification sent)
Given I am using a screen reader, When the alert notification is received, Then the utilisation percentage, severity, and suggested action are announced accessibly
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)
stories.reasoning: Distinct alert type with specific trigger logic and threshold configuration. Utilisation is 30% of score weighting.

stories.title: Configure Custom Utilisation Thresholds
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to customise my utilisation alert thresholds, so that I receive alerts at levels meaningful to my situation.
acceptance_criteria: Given I access alert settings, When I adjust utilisation thresholds, Then I can set custom percentages for advisory, warning, and urgent levels
Given I set custom thresholds, When a threshold is breached, Then alerts fire at my custom levels instead of defaults
Given I am using a screen reader, When navigating threshold settings, Then all sliders/inputs are accessible with current values announced
stories.reasoning: Customisation layer on top of the alert. Separate from core alert logic for incremental delivery.

stories.title: Alert on Upcoming Payments at Risk of Being Missed
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to be warned when an upcoming payment might be missed due to insufficient funds, so that I can avoid credit score damage.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Direct Debit is due within 3 days AND insufficient funds are detected in the payment account, When the risk is identified, Then I receive a push notification and in-app message within 15 minutes
Given the alert is delivered, When I view it, Then it shows payment amount, due date, creditor name, estimated score impact of a missed payment, and a suggested action
Given the alert tone, When displayed, Then it uses empathetic language with no blame (e.g., 'You have a payment coming up that might need attention' not 'You are about to miss a payment')
Given I am in Breathing Space, When a payment alert would normally trigger, Then it is suppressed
Given I am using a screen reader, When the payment alert is received, Then all details are announced accessibly
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.regulatory_linkage: CONC 7 (treat customers fairly — no pressure language)
stories.reasoning: Preventive alert requiring integration with payment schedule data. Payment history is 35% of score weighting — highest impact factor.

stories.title: Notify When Score Improvement Unlocks New Product Eligibility
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to know when my improved score unlocks new lending products, so that I can see the tangible benefits of my credit improvement efforts.
acceptance_criteria: Given my score crosses a product eligibility threshold on refresh, When the eligibility change is detected, Then I receive a celebratory notification stating the product now available and what changed (e.g., 'Your score moved to Good band — you now qualify for our Personal Loan')
Given multiple products become available on the same refresh, When notifications are generated, Then only one eligibility notification is sent per refresh cycle (highest-value product)
Given I am flagged as in financial distress, When an eligibility notification would trigger, Then it is suppressed
Given I am using a screen reader, When the eligibility notification is received, Then the product name and achievement are announced accessibly
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.regulatory_linkage: CONC 3 (financial promotion — if linking to offer), FCA Consumer Duty
stories.reasoning: Connects score improvement to tangible product access. Distinct from offer presentation (EP-06) — this is the notification trigger.

stories.title: Configure Alert Preferences and Opt-Out
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to control which alerts I receive, how often, and through which channels, so that I'm not overwhelmed by notifications.
acceptance_criteria: Given I access Settings > Credit Coach > Alert Preferences, When I view the configuration screen, Then I can toggle each alert type (utilisation/payment/eligibility), set frequency caps (e.g., max 2 per week), choose channels (push/in-app), and set quiet hours
Given I opt out of a specific alert type, When the trigger condition is met, Then no notification is sent for that type (but other types still fire if enabled)
Given I change preferences, When I save, Then changes take effect immediately for subsequent alerts
Given alert opt-out, When I disable all alerts, Then CRA consent and data retrieval are NOT affected (only alert delivery is stopped)
Given I am using a screen reader, When navigating preference toggles, Then each toggle's current state and label are announced
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR (right to object to direct marketing communications)

requirements_used: FR-24 — Alert Preference Configuration and Opt-Out

stories.reasoning: Customer control feature. Separate from alert logic as it's a cross-cutting preference layer.

stories.title: Handle Alert Delivery Failure and Retry
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want alerts to be reliably delivered even if the first attempt fails, so that I don't miss important credit health notifications.
acceptance_criteria: Given a push notification fails to deliver (device offline, token expired), When the delivery fails, Then the system retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff (1min, 5min, 30min)
Given all retry attempts fail, When the customer next opens the app, Then undelivered alerts are shown in an in-app notification centre with timestamps
Given an alert is triggered but the customer has opted out of that alert type, When the alert engine processes the event, Then the alert is suppressed and no delivery is attempted
Then status changes are announced to assistive technology via live regions
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (consumer support — customers must receive timely information)

requirements_used: FR-24 — Alert Preference Configuration and Opt-Out

stories.reasoning: Error path for alert delivery. NFR-04 requires delivery within 15 minutes — retry logic ensures this SLA is met even on first failure. In-app fallback ensures no alert is permanently lost. 3 points: retry logic + in-app notification centre + opt-out enforcement.

stories.title: Delete Score History and Opt Out of Credit Coach
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want to delete my stored credit data and opt out of the Credit Coach at any time, so that I can exercise my right to erasure.
acceptance_criteria: Given I navigate to Settings > Credit Coach > Delete my data, When I view the deletion screen, Then it clearly explains what will be deleted (score history, action plans, simulation history, alert history) and what will be retained (consent records for compliance, offer audit trail for 6 years per FCA)
Given I confirm deletion, When the process executes, Then CRA data is purged, coaching data is purged, the agent is deregistered for my account, and consent is marked as withdrawn (retained)
Given deletion is irreversible, When the confirmation screen is shown, Then a clear warning states this cannot be undone and I can re-enrol later with a fresh start
Given I am using a screen reader, When navigating the deletion flow, Then all warnings, explanations, and confirmation buttons are accessible
Given deletion is processed, When the system updates, Then an audit log records the deletion event with timestamp for compliance
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure), FCA SYSC 9.1.1R (retention exceptions)

requirements_used: FR-30 — Right to Erasure of Score History

stories.reasoning: UK GDPR Art. 17 obligation. Complex deletion logic with retention exceptions for regulatory compliance.

stories.title: Explain Automated Decision Logic for Pre-Approved Offers
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want to understand how automated decisions about my pre-approved offers were made, so that I can assess whether the decision is fair.
acceptance_criteria: Given a pre-approved offer is displayed, When I tap 'How was this decided?', Then I see a plain English explanation (Flesch >= 60) of: which factors led to this offer, what it means for me, and what happens if I accept or decline
Given the explanation is shown, When I want human review, Then a 'Request human review' button is available that triggers escalation to the lending team within 5 business days
Given I request human review, When the request is submitted, Then I receive confirmation with expected timeline and reference number
Given I am using a screen reader, When viewing the automated decision explanation, Then all logic, significance, and consequence information is announced accessibly
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR Art. 22 (automated decision-making), DUAA 2025 (high-risk ADM)

requirements_used: FR-31 — Automated Decision Explanation and Human Review

stories.reasoning: Legal requirement for automated decisions with significant effect. On-demand explanation layer over offer display.

stories.title: Detect Vulnerability Indicators and Signpost Debt Advice
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want the system to detect when I may be in financial difficulty and provide me with free debt advice contacts, so that I get support when I need it most.
acceptance_criteria: Given vulnerability indicators are detected (high utilisation >90% sustained, multiple missed payments, gambling transactions, payday loan usage, sudden income drop, returned Direct Debits), When the detection runs on score refresh, Then a vulnerability flag is set on my profile

Given I am flagged as vulnerable, When I access the Credit Coach, Then promotional content is suppressed, tone is adjusted to empathetic, and free debt advice is displayed: StepChange (0800 138 1111), Citizens Advice, National Debtline (0808 808 4000)
Given I am flagged as vulnerable, When viewing any Credit Coach screen, Then a persistent 'Need help?' option is visible connecting to human support
Given I am using a screen reader, When debt advice signposting is displayed, Then phone numbers and service names are announced clearly
Given vulnerability detection data is classified Restricted, When processed, Then it is encrypted and access is restricted to authorised services only
stories.regulatory_linkage: CONC 7.3.7AR (debt advice signposting), FCA Consumer Duty (consumer support outcome)

requirements_used: FR-36 — Vulnerability Detection and Debt Advice Signposting

stories.reasoning: Regulatory requirement under CONC 7. Complex detection logic with multiple data sources and significant UX implications.

stories.title: Suppress Offers During Financial Distress
stories.user_story: As a compliance officer, I want all pre-approved offers automatically suppressed when financial distress indicators are detected, so that we do not encourage over-borrowing for vulnerable customers.
acceptance_criteria: Given a vulnerability flag is active OR Breathing Space is active OR the customer is in arrears on Lloyds products OR debt-to-income exceeds safe threshold, When the suppression check runs, Then all pre-approved offers are immediately suppressed
Given offers are suppressed, When distress indicators clear, Then offers resume automatically on the next refresh cycle
Given suppression is active, When the customer attempts to view offers, Then no offers are shown (no manual override available — protective measure)
Given suppression occurs, When the audit log is updated, Then it records suppression reason, start timestamp, and duration
Given suppression data is classified Restricted, When logged, Then access is restricted to compliance and operations teams
Then all form inputs have associated labels readable by screen readers
stories.regulatory_linkage: FCA Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm), CONC 7

requirements_used: FR-37 — Suppress Offers During Financial Distress
domain_kb_sections: PD12 — Consumer Duty avoid foreseeable harm

stories.reasoning: Distinct from vulnerability detection — this is the enforcement action (suppression) triggered by detection.

stories.title: Implement Breathing Space Compliance Freeze
stories.user_story: As a compliance officer, I want the system to automatically freeze all credit-related communications and charges when a customer enters Breathing Space, so that we comply with the Debt Respite Scheme Regulations 2020.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Breathing Space notification is received (via Insolvency Service portal or internal collections flag), When the status is activated, Then all credit-related alerts are frozen, pre-approved offers suppressed, and proactive communications stopped
Given Standard Breathing Space (60 days), When the period expires, Then normal service resumes automatically and frozen charges are NOT retrospectively applied
Given Mental Health Crisis Breathing Space, When active, Then it lasts for the duration of treatment + 30 days with the same protections
Given Breathing Space is active, When any action is taken or suppressed, Then a complete audit trail records all actions during the period
Given Breathing Space data is classified Restricted, When stored, Then it is encrypted and access-logged
Then status changes are announced to assistive technology via live regions
stories.regulatory_linkage: Debt Respite Scheme Regulations 2020, CONC 7

domain_kb_sections: PD7 — Breathing Space Debt Respite Scheme 2021

stories.reasoning: Legal obligation under Debt Respite Scheme Regulations 2020. Specific legal triggers and durations distinct from general distress suppression.

stories.title: Support DSAR Export of All Credit Coaching Data
stories.user_story: As a retail banking customer, I want to export all data the Credit Coach holds about me, so that I can exercise my right of access under GDPR.
acceptance_criteria: Given a DSAR is received, When the Credit Coach data extraction runs, Then it exports: score history, action plans (current and archived), simulation history, offers presented and actions taken, consent records, alert history, and preference settings
Given the export is generated, When delivered, Then it is available in both machine-readable (JSON) and human-readable (PDF) formats

Given the GDPR timeline, When a DSAR is received, Then the Credit Coach data subset is provided to the central DSAR team within 5 business days (allowing time for the 1 calendar month overall deadline)
Given I am using a screen reader, When I request my data via the app, Then the request flow and confirmation are accessible
Given DSAR export data is classified Restricted, When generated and transmitted, Then it is encrypted in transit and access-logged
stories.regulatory_linkage: GDPR Art. 15 (right of access / DSAR), 1 calendar month deadline

requirements_used: FR-49 — Data Subject Access Request Support

stories.reasoning: GDPR Art. 15 obligation. Cross-cutting data aggregation requirement across all Credit Coach data stores.

stories.title: Provide Human Escalation Path from Any Screen
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to speak to a human adviser at any point, so that I can get help with complex situations the AI cannot handle.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on any Credit Coach screen, When I look for human support, Then a persistent 'Speak to someone' option is visible and accessible
Given I tap 'Speak to someone', When the escalation options appear, Then I can choose: live chat transfer, callback request, or branch appointment booking
Given I escalate to a human, When the handoff occurs, Then conversation context (history, score summary, active plan) is passed to the human adviser
Given escalation SLAs, When I choose live chat, Then connection occurs within 5 minutes during business hours; when I choose callback, Then it occurs within 24 hours
Given I am using a screen reader, When the escalation options are displayed, Then all options and their descriptions are announced accessibly
Then personally identifiable information is not logged in application logs and API responses do not leak data to unauthorised sessions
stories.regulatory_linkage: PRA FS2/23 (human oversight), FCA Consumer Duty (consumer support)
stories.reasoning: FS2/23 regulatory expectation for human oversight. Cross-cutting feature available from all screens.

stories.title: Display Multi-Bureau Score Comparison
stories.user_story: As a credit coach user, I want to compare my scores across multiple credit bureaux side-by-side, so that I understand why scores differ between agencies.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have consented to multiple CRAs, When scores from multiple bureaux are available, Then I see each bureau's score on its native scale, band per bureau, and key differences in factors between them
Given scores differ between bureaux, When the comparison is displayed, Then a plain English explanation of why scores differ is provided
Given only one CRA is available, When the comparison screen is accessed, Then it shows the single score with a note that comparison requires additional bureau access
Given I am using a screen reader, When the multi-bureau comparison is displayed, Then each bureau's score, band, and differences are announced in logical order
stories.reasoning: Nice-to-Have feature from Phase 5 roadmap. Builds on existing multi-CRA infrastructure with minimal additional effort.
